## New

- **Local history** — every finished session is now auto-saved and can be reopened later. Live meetings save their audio (Ogg/Opus) on stop; uploaded recordings are kept alongside their analysis. A new **History** window (open it from the header) lists past sessions as cards; click one to reload it into the replay view with its analysis already restored — no re-run, no re-upload. Closes the old gap where a live meeting was lost the moment you hit Stop.

## Fixes

- **Onboarding** — if you skipped first-run setup, the speaker-diarization model now downloads on demand from Settings instead of leaving "Diarize by voice" broken.

## macOS — how to open

This build is **ad-hoc signed** (not yet notarized with an Apple Developer ID), so on first open macOS shows a security prompt:

- **Right-click (or Control-click) the app → Open → Open**, or
- **System Settings → Privacy & Security → Open Anyway** (after the first blocked attempt).

If a download still shows "damaged", clear the quarantine once:

```
xattr -cr /Applications/Parley.app
```
